audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Achan, Ai defeat, lot exposure, confession, and Valley of Achor execution narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for corporate trespass, failed attack, thirty-six deaths, Joshua's lament, lot-based identification, Achan's confession, concealed plunder, execution of household and animals, stone heap, and collective-punishment concerns.

verses.restored: Children of Israel acted unfaithfully concerning devoted thing: Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah, from tribe of Judah, took from devoted thing; and anger of YHWH burned against children of Israel.
Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai, which is beside Beth-aven, east of Bethel, and spoke to them, saying, Go up and spy out land. The men went up and spied out Ai.
They returned to Joshua and said to him, Let not all people go up; about two thousand or three thousand men may go up and strike Ai. Do not weary all people there, for they are few.
So from people about three thousand men went up there, and they fled before men of Ai.
Men of Ai struck from them about thirty-six men and pursued them before gate as far as Shebarim and struck them on descent; heart of people melted and became as water.
Joshua tore his garments and fell upon his face to earth before ark of YHWH until evening, he and elders of Israel, and they put dust upon their heads.
Joshua said, Ah, Lord YHWH, why did you bring this people across Jordan at all, to give us into hand of Amorite to destroy us? If only we had been content and dwelt across Jordan!
Please, Lord, what shall I say after Israel has turned its back before enemies?
Canaanite and all inhabitants of land will hear and surround us and cut off our name from earth; and what will you do for your great name?
YHWH said to Joshua, Rise for yourself! Why is this, that you fall upon your face?
Israel has sinned/misaligned, and they also crossed my covenant that I commanded them; they took from devoted thing, and also stole, and also deceived, and also put it among their own vessels.

Therefore children of Israel cannot stand before their enemies; they turn their back before their enemies because they have become devoted to destruction. I will not continue to be with you unless you destroy devoted thing from your midst.

Rise, sanctify people, and say, Sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, for thus says YHWH Divine of Israel: A devoted thing is in your midst, Israel; you cannot stand before your enemies until you remove devoted thing from your midst.
You shall draw near in morning by your tribes; tribe YHWH takes shall draw near by clans, clan YHWH takes shall draw near by households, and household YHWH takes shall draw near man by man.
It shall be, the one taken with devoted thing shall be burned with fire, he and all belonging to him, because he crossed covenant of YHWH and because he did disgraceful folly in Israel.
Joshua rose early in morning and brought Israel near by tribes, and tribe of Judah was taken.
He brought clan of Judah near, and clan of Zerahites was taken; he brought clan of Zerahites near man by man, and Zabdi was taken.
He brought his household near man by man, and Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah, from tribe of Judah, was taken.
Joshua said to Achan, My son, please give glory to YHWH Divine of Israel and give praise/confession to him; tell me, please, what you did; do not hide it from me.
Achan answered Joshua and said, Truly I have sinned/misaligned against YHWH Divine of Israel, and thus and thus I did.

I saw among spoil a good mantle of Shinar, two hundred shekels of silver, and one tongue/bar of gold, fifty shekels its weight; I coveted them and took them. Look, they are hidden in earth in midst of my tent, and silver beneath it.
Joshua sent messengers, and they ran to tent; and look, it was hidden in his tent, with silver beneath it.
They took them from midst of tent and brought them to Joshua and all children of Israel and poured/set them out before YHWH.

Joshua and all Israel with him took Achan son of Zerah, silver, mantle, tongue/bar of gold, his sons, his daughters, his ox, his donkey, his flock, his tent, and all belonging to him, and brought them up to Valley of Achor/Trouble.
Joshua said, Why have you troubled us? YHWH will trouble you this day. All Israel stoned him with stones; they burned them with fire and stoned them with stones.
They raised over him a great heap of stones, to this day; and YHWH turned from burning of his anger. Therefore name of that place is called Valley of Achor/Trouble to this day.

translation_notes: The narrative opens by assigning one person's act to all Israel, demonstrating a strong corporate-responsibility model.
The failed attack was launched on scouts' confidence with no recorded inquiry of YHWH, and thirty-six soldiers die before the hidden theft is identified.
The lot process narrows tribe, clan, household, and individual, but the text does not explain its mechanics or provide modern evidentiary safeguards.
Achan confesses taking a Shinar mantle, silver, and gold from Jericho's devoted spoil. His confession does not ethically justify punishment of uninvolved relatives.

Verse 24 explicitly brings Achan's sons, daughters, animals, tent, and possessions to the execution site. Verse 25 shifts between singular and plural, strongly suggesting the household and animals were destroyed with him, though interpreters debate details.
The killing of children and animals for one adult's theft is collective punishment and must be categorically rejected as modern justice.
The community that executed Jericho now punishes private appropriation of sacred-war plunder; this internal consistency does not make the larger herem system ethical.
Achor wordplay links Achan's troubling of Israel to the valley's name and later prophetic traditions of hope require separate cross-reference.
safety_note: Never use Joshua 7 to justify collective punishment, execution of children, destruction of families, trial by lot, coercive confession, killing for theft, or leader-controlled purges. Center transparent accountability, individual responsibility, reliable evidence, restitution where possible, and categorical protection of relatives, dependents, and animals from another person's wrongdoing.
